question_text: Explain why ultraviolet (UV) radiation can be dangerous to human skin.
correct_answer: UV radiation has high energy that can damage skin cells and DNA, causing sunburn and increasing the risk of skin cancer. It is ionising radiation.
explanation: UV radiation is high-energy electromagnetic radiation that can penetrate the outer layers of skin and damage the cells beneath. This can cause immediate damage (sunburn) and long-term damage (skin cancer) because it has enough energy to remove electrons from atoms (ionise them).
